config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but it should not automatically come before individual rights, because once you let “society” override the person too easily, abuse becomes very easy to justify. From my perspective, a healthy society protects basic rights first and only limits them in narrow, carefully justified cases. In practice, I trust systems that treat individual dignity as the foundation, not as something society can discard whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of broad claim makes me uneasy because history shows how easily governments and majorities can use “the public good” to excuse abuse, discrimination, or overreach. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and shared rules do matter, but those limits need to be narrow, justified, and accountable. I would put it this way: society works best when it protects individual rights first, rather than treating them as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand why people emphasize harmony and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to silence criticism, weaken minorities, or excuse unfair treatment. Society does matter, but it is strongest when it protects the dignity and rights of each person instead of treating individuals as sacrifices for vague collective goals. I would only accept limits on rights in narrow cases with clear justification, not as a default principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, that framing is too dangerous because it gives majorities and institutions too much room to claim they are acting for the common good while trampling dissent, minorities, or unpopular people. I do think society can and should limit individual rights in narrow cases like public safety, fraud, or preventing direct harm, but those limits have to be specific, accountable, and hard to abuse. In my view, a free society is strongest when individual rights are the starting point, not something society gets to override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because in my view society should not be given a blank check to override individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony and collective duty, but I have also seen how easily “the common good” can become a tool to pressure dissenters, minorities, or ordinary people with less power. That is why I put basic rights first, with only narrow and clearly justified limits when there is real harm or public danger. A society that asks individuals to sacrifice too much too easily is not strong; it is just convenient for those already in control.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that idea sets off alarms because American history is full of examples where “the public good” was used to justify oppression, censorship, and discrimination against unpopular groups. Society does matter, but I would only accept limits on individual rights when they are narrowly tailored, clearly necessary, and accountable to prevent real harm. A society that treats rights as optional is not protecting the common good; it is giving power too much room to abuse people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
